title: baeckea cunninghamii
intro: Baeckea cunninghamii, also known as the 'Duckberry', is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible red berries. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Baeckea cunninghamii include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, often used topically to apply the plant's medicinal properties directly to affected areas.
